title: Calculating Age
text: Calculating Age A common requirement is to calculate the age of a person in years. In the following example, the user enters a birth date in the BIRTHDATE field, and the resulting age (in years) is displayed in the AGE field. BIRTHDATE—editable field with data type date . AGE—field with data type numeric . If the current date is 21-may-2008 and the BIRTHDATE is 21-may-1992, the expected AGE is 16. In Uniface, dates are represented in the format ccyymmddhhmmtt . Although you might expect 16 years to be 00160000000000 , this is not the case. Instead, it is 00151231000000 , that is 15 years, 12 months, and 31 days (because 2008 is a leap year; otherwise it would be 30 days). If you try to calculate the age only on the basis of the yy part of the data, the following expression would result in AGE = 15, instead of 16: AGE = ($date - BIRTHDATE)[y] Copy You must therefore, check the mm and dd parts of the result as well. variables date vCurrDate date vPastDate date vElapsedDate boolean vIsLeapYear endvariables vCurrDate = $date vPastDate = BIRTHDATE vElapsedDate = vCurrDate - vPastDate ;Check the month and date to see if another year ;should be added to the displayed AGE. ;This is only required if mm=12 and dd=31 or dd=30 if ((vElapsedDate[m]=12) & (vElapsedDate[d]=31)) ;leap year AGE=(vElapsedDate[y]) + 1 ;add 1 year elseif ((vElapsedDate[m]=12) & (vElapsedDate[d]=30)) ;normal year, or one leap year minus one day ;Check if vCurrDate is leap year. A year is a leap year if: ; - it is divisible by 4, but not by 100. ; - it is divisible by 4, by 100, and by 400. if (vCurrDate%4 = 0) if (vCurrDate%100 = 0) if (vCurrDate%400 = 0) vIsLeapYear = "T" endif endif endif ;If it's a normal year: if (vIsLeapYear = "F") AGE=(vElapsedDate[y]) ;If its a leap year: else AGE=(vElapsedDate[y]) + 1 else AGE=(vElapsedDate[y]) endif Copy Related Links Date and Time Data Uniface offers a wide variety of data types for date and time values. Arithmetic With Date and Time Values To perform arithmetic with date and time values, Uniface converts these values internally to the equivalent number of days. Extracting Values From Date and Time Data When you are working with data stored as a Date, a Time, or a Datetime, you can extract information such as the week number from a date, the month name from a date, or the number of minutes in a particular time. The type of data to be extracted is specified by the parameter. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
